mission.title: apisix: Proposal: support OpenPolicyAgent for access control
mission.description: Proposal: support OpenPolicyAgent for access control. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current apisix deployment
steps.description:
Verify your apisix version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix
helm list -n apisix 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working apisix installation.

steps.title: Review apisix configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant apisix configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix
kubectl get configmap -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=apisix
```
OPA is an open source lightweight general-purpose policy engine, which is a full-featured policy engine that can replace the built-in policy module in your software and decouple the service from the policy engine.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Proposal: support OpenPolicyAgent for access control
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix
kubectl get events -n apisix --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Proposal: support OpenPolicyAgent for access control" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: The OPA plugin for access control was added to Apache APISIX via PR #5734. Configure it on routes using the `opa` plugin with `conf.host` pointing to your OPA server and `conf.policy` specifying the policy path.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with apisix installed or the issue environment reproducible.
